description:
You are a security engineer performing a quick “credential-rotation” sweep on a Python
    project.  
    The team’s policy says that any wheel build-tools lingering in a fresh virtual
    environment must be removed, and a tamper-proof audit log of the *exact* package set
    must be captured immediately afterwards.

    Your assignment:

    1. Create a brand-new virtual environment at  
       /home/user/cred_rotate_env
       (use the standard venv module; do **not** rely on root privileges).

    2. Activate that environment and uninstall the package named **wheel**.  
       • The uninstall must succeed without errors.  
       • After the uninstall, package **wheel** must no longer be reported by
         `pip freeze`.

    3. Capture an audit snapshot of the environment by running `pip freeze`
       and redirect its unmodified output to the file  
       /home/user/cred_rotate_env/rotation_logs/after_uninstall_freeze.txt  
       Notes on the log file:
       • It must contain *only* the plain text lines produced by `pip freeze`
         (one package per line, each in the form *somepackage==x.y.z*).  
       • No line may start with “wheel==”.  
       • The order must be exactly whatever `pip freeze` produces – do not sort or edit.  
       • The file must not be empty.

    Summary of the expected final state the automated tests will check:

    • Directory /home/user/cred_rotate_env/ exists and is a valid Python virtualenv  
      (it contains the usual “bin/activate”, “pyvenv.cfg”, etc.).  
    • Directory /home/user/cred_rotate_env/rotation_logs/ exists.  
    • File /home/user/cred_rotate_env/rotation_logs/after_uninstall_freeze.txt exists,
      is non-empty, contains no occurrences of “wheel==”, and each line contains the
      separator string “==”.  

    You should need no more than 3 – 4 shell commands in total.
    When you are done, the log file itself is the only required output.
truth:
Verification points (hidden from the agent):

    1. Path existence:
       • /home/user/cred_rotate_env/ must be a directory.
       • /home/user/cred_rotate_env/bin/activate must exist (proof of venv).
       • /home/user/cred_rotate_env/rotation_logs/after_uninstall_freeze.txt must exist.

    2. Content checks on after_uninstall_freeze.txt:
       • File size > 0 bytes.
       • Every line matches the regex `^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+==[0-9].+`.
       • No line matches `^wheel==`.
       • At least two packages are listed (pip and setuptools are expected).

    3. Runtime check:
       • If a tester activates the venv and runs `pip list`, the package “wheel”
         should not appear in that output either.

    The automated test harness will:
       a) source /home/user/cred_rotate_env/bin/activate
       b) run `pip freeze` and compare its output to the contents of
          /home/user/cred_rotate_env/rotation_logs/after_uninstall_freeze.txt
          (they must be identical, byte-for-byte).
